entries.text: Land at Narita
Clear immigration + customs
Buy Suica card, buy N'EX ticket at JR counter, withdraw cash at 7-Eleven ATM
Board Narita Express (N'EX) to Shibuya
Arrive Shibuya Station
Walk to Shibuya Granbell Hotel (10 min)
Check in, drop bags, freshen up, change
Walk to Gyukatsu Motomura Shibuya (5-10 min from hotel)
Queue at Motomura (~30-60 min Fri evening)
Eat gyukatsu (beef cutlet, DIY grilling)
Walk to Grandfather's listening bar (10 min)
Grandfather's — vinyl + highball, decompress from flight
Walk to Nonbei Yokocho (5 min)
One drink at a tiny bar in Nonbei Yokocho
Walk back to hotel
Crash (or earlier — no shame after a transpacific flight)
Wake up (jet lag is your alarm clock)
Leave hotel, walk to Shibuya Station (10 min)
Ginza Line to Tsukiji (20 min)
Tsukiji Outer Market food crawl — tamagoyaki, scallops, uni, sashimi, tuna skewers
Optional: Masamoto knives at market edge
Walk from Tsukiji to Ginza (15 min)
Chopstick Making Workshop — Ginza Hashikura (~60 min) ⚠️ BOOKED 10:00 AM
Walk around Ginza / optional Grand Seiko Boutique (Brad solo flex)
Subway Ginza → Shinjuku-sanchome (15 min) for Nakajima
Queue at Nakajima (~30-45 min for 12:30 wave)
Eat Michelin sardine lunch (990 yen!!)
(B) Skip Nakajima if stuffed from Tsukiji → graze Ginza Six depachika
Subway back toward Shibuya
Walk to Houseki Hiroba Shibuya (5 min from hotel area)
Houseki Hiroba Shibuya — secondhand watch browsing. Rolex, Grand Seiko, Omega. Massive inventory. (2 hrs)
Walk back to hotel, freshen up, grab coffee
Free time — rest at hotel, explore Shibuya
Walk to Shibuya Scramble Square (5 min from hotel)
Check in at Shibuya Sky, go through escalators to observation deck
Shibuya Sky — booked entry at 6:20 PM ⚠️ BOOKED ✅. Golden hour + city lights. Sunset ~6:23 PM.
Come down, experience Shibuya Crossing at street level
JR Yamanote Shibuya → Shinjuku (5 min + walk to Takashimaya)
Katsukura Shinjuku Takashimaya 14F — Kyoto-style tonkatsu (LO 9 PM, walk-in)
(B) Skip Shinjuku → eat at Scramble Square: Shabu-Shabu Tsukada 12F or USHIHACHI yakiniku
(B) Gyukatsu Motomura Shibuya — 10 min walk, 30-60 min queue
JR Yamanote back to Shibuya (5 min)
JBS Vinyl Bar — vinyl, whisky, no cover (5 min walk)
SG Club — craft cocktails (Guzzle floor or Sip basement, 5 min walk)
Walk back to hotel (10 min)
Wake up
Leave hotel, train Shibuya → Nakameguro (Tokyu Toyoko, 4 min)
Arrive Nakameguro
Walk to Onibus Coffee (5 min) — pour-over coffee
Walk to I'm Donut? Nakameguro
Queue at I'm Donut? (~30-40 min before 10am opening)
Buy + eat donuts ⚠️ ARRIVE BY 9:30 — sells out by afternoon
Walk along Meguro River to Daikanyama (15 min)
Browse Daikanyama T-Site (Tsutaya Books), Okura Daikanyama
Walk back toward Nakameguro
Arrive Udatsu Sushi ⚠️ BOOKED 12:00 PM — Smart casual: no tees/shorts/sandals
Michelin sushi omakase lunch (~90 min)
(B) Sushi SHIN in Nishiazabu if Udatsu full
Walk/browse Nakameguro
Train Nakameguro → Shin-Toyosu (Yurikamome, ~40 min with transfer)
Walk to teamLab Planets, store bags in lockers, remove shoes
Enter teamLab Planets ⚠️ BOOK ~3:00 PM SLOT (arrive early)
teamLab Planets (~2-2.5 hrs)
Retrieve bags, dry off feet, put shoes back on
Optional: #FFFFFFT white tee store — open Sun 12-6pm, 10 min walk from Omotesando
Train Shin-Toyosu → Omotesando area (~35 min)
Freshen up / walk around Omotesando
Arrive Ukaitei Omotesando ⚠️ BOOKED 7:00 PM — dress nice-ish
Teppanyaki dinner — private room, dedicated chef (~2 hrs)
(B) Ebisu Yokocho — izakaya stall hopping if teppanyaki too much
Train Omotesando → Shimokitazawa (via Shibuya, 10 min)
Little Soul Cafe — soul/funk vinyl
Check Basement Bar schedule for live music
Train back to Shibuya (3 min)
Hotel
Wake up
Leave hotel, subway Shibuya → Jimbocho (Hanzomon Line, 15 min)
Arrive Jimbocho
Glitch Coffee — 2-3 bean tasting flight (45-60 min)
Walk toward Udon Maruka (5 min)
Browse Jimbocho bookshops while waiting (Maruka queue starts ~10am)
Queue at Udon Maruka (~30-45 min for 11am opening) ⚠️ LAST CHANCE — closed GW
Eat kamaage udon (cash only)
Walk/subway to Kappabashi Street (20 min)
Knife shopping — Tsubaya, Kamata Hakensha (1-1.5 hrs)
If Ginza Hachigo reserved:
Subway Kappabashi area → Ginza (15 min)
Eat at Ginza Hachigo ⚠️ RESERVATION — truffle-foie gras ramen
If NOT reserved — Nakiryu tantanmen:
Subway toward Asakusa (5 min — Kappabashi is basically Asakusa)
Sensoji Temple — Kaminarimon gate, Nakamise street, temple grounds (1.5 hrs)
Explore Asakusa backstreets
Subway Asakusa → Shibuya (30 min) — rest at hotel
Rest / freshen up
Subway Shibuya → Kagurazaka/Iidabashi (20 min)
Walk the lantern-lit alleys of Kagurazaka
Find Shinpo izakaya (look for blue noren curtains)
Dinner at Shinpo — crab croquettes, smoked daikon, sake
Subway Kagurazaka → Shinjuku (10 min)
Jazz Bar Samurai (5F, Shinjuku 3-chome) — vinyl jazz + 2,500 maneki-neko
Option A: Shinjuku Pit Inn — jazz evening show (~10pm late set)
Option B: JR Chuo to Koenji (10 min) → UFO Club for psych rock
Option C: Head home — it's been a full day
Last train window. Head to Shibuya.
Hotel
Wake up
Leave hotel, walk to Meiji Shrine through Yoyogi Park (10 min)
Meiji Shrine — peaceful walk, main hall, forest path (45 min)
Walk to Harajuku / Takeshita-dori (10 min)
Browse Harajuku — Takeshita-dori window shopping
Walk to Harajuku Gyoza Ru
Gyoza snack — best gyoza in Tokyo (~500-800 yen)
Walk to Mofusand Harajuku (Yeva's gifts), browse more shops
Walk to Koffee Mameya Omotesando (opens 10am)
Coffee sommelier experience
Walk to Henry's Burger OR Shin Udon (both in Harajuku, ~5 min apart)
Lunch — PICK ONE or split:
A) Henry's Burger Harajuku — A5 wagyu burgers. Get the HARAMI BURGER (~3,810 yen) ⚠️ DM @henrysburger_japan 1 WEEK AHEAD for harami reservation. Right on Meiji-dori. Owner speaks English.
B) Shin Udon Harajuku — carbonara udon (950 yen, walk-in)
Free time / browse Harajuku area
Free time / rest
Free time — extra rest time, explore Harajuku more, or early return to hotel
Head back to hotel area
Drop by hotel / freshen up
Walk to Midori Sushi Shibuya
Queue at Midori (~60-90 min GW wait) ⚠️ ARRIVE BY 5:30-6PM
Eat sushi dinner
(B) If line is insane → pivot to Ebisu Yokocho (1 stop on Yamanote)
Walk back toward hotel area
Option A: Ginza Music Bar (subway to Ginza, 15 min) — til 4am
Option B: JBS Vinyl Bar (walking distance) — no cover, til 11pm
Option C: Early night — Golden Week starts tomorrow, save energy
Back to hotel
Wake up
Quick coffee at Streamer Coffee Shibuya (opens 8am)
Final pack, grab bags (or they're already at Kyoto via takkyubin)
Leave hotel, walk to Shibuya Station (10 min)
JR Yamanote Line Shibuya → Tokyo Station (25 min)
Navigate to Shinkansen platforms (14-19), buy ekiben bento
Board Nozomi 23 ⚠️ BOOKED — don't miss this train
Shinkansen to Kyoto — eat ekiben, enjoy the scenery, watch for Mt. Fuji (right side)
Arrive Kyoto Station
Karasuma Line subway Kyoto → Shijo (4 min)
Walk from Shijo Station Exit 11 to Hotel Forza (7 min)
Check in, drop bags, freshen up
Walk to Nishiki Market (5 min north of hotel)
Nishiki Market food crawl — tako tamago, tempura skewers, yuba croquette, doughnuts
Walk to Teramachi Shopping District
Kuoe Watches — custom vintage-inspired watch (~$290)
Optional: Kyoto Loft (Mina Kyoto building, same street)
SOU SOU Kyoto (Shinkyogoku-dori, 1 min from Kawaramachi Stn) — modern haori jackets, bold textile prints (~¥14-28k). Right between Nishiki and Teramachi. Quick browse or full stop.
Walk back to hotel, rest
Walk to Honke Owariya (15 min) OR stay near hotel for Omen Pontocho
Lunch was Nishiki grazing; dinner is the sit-down:
Honke Owariya — 5-tier Horai Soba in a 560-year-old building
(B) Omen Pontocho — Kyoto's beloved udon (walking distance)
(B) Wagyu Ramen Julia — quick wagyu broth ramen in Gion
Walk east along Shijo-dori into Gion at dusk
Gion evening stroll — Hanamikoji-dori, teahouses, possible maiko sighting
Walk to Pontocho alley
Browse Pontocho — atmospheric narrow alley, peek at river terraces
P.M. Sounds — vinyl bar on Kiyamachi (near hotel)
Walk back to hotel (5 min)
Wake up
Leave hotel, subway to Kyoto Station (4 min)
JR Nara Line to Inari Station (5 min, 1 stop)
Walk out of station — shrine is right there
Fushimi Inari — Senbon Torii (vermillion tunnel), first section (15-20 min flat walk)
Walk south toward Fushimi sake district (10 min)
Sake tasting flight at a Fushimi brewery (~1,000-1,500 yen)
Train back to hotel area
Weekenders Coffee Tominokoji — THE Kyoto coffee
Walk east along Shijo-dori toward Gion
Yasaka Shrine (free, quick visit)
Walk south through Ninenzaka + Sannenzaka — stone lanes, shops, tea houses
Kiyomizu-dera — UNESCO site, famous wooden stage (~400 yen) ⚠️ uphill but steady
Walk back down through Higashiyama shops
Walk to Kitenya tempura (central location)
Lunch — Bib Gourmand tempura (~2,000-4,000 yen)
(B) Yoshikawa Tempura — machiya with koi pond (reserve ahead, ~5,000-8,000 yen)
Walk back toward hotel

⚠️ KICHI KICHI ATTEMPT — both phones on kichikichi.com/kichikichi-reservation at 12:59. Refresh at 1:00. Slots fill in 10-15 sec. If you score dinner, swap tonight's ramen plan. Try EVERY day May 1-3.
Rest at hotel — you started at 6:45am
Walk to Ippodo Tea (Teramachi, 10 min)
Ippodo Tea — whisk matcha, seasonal sweets, buy matcha to bring home
Walk to Assemblages Kakimoto (if booked)
Matcha parfait at Assemblages ⚠️ BOOK AHEAD — closes 5pm
Rest / freshen up at hotel
Walk/subway toward Ichijoji OR stay in Gion
If Menya Inoichi (Ichijoji):
Train to Ichijoji (Eizan Railway from Demachiyanagi, ~20 min)
Walk to Menya Inoichi, queue (~15-20 min)
Eat Bib Gourmand seafood shio ramen
Train back to hotel area
(B) Wagyu Ramen Julia in Gion — walking distance, no train needed
Walk to Pontocho area
L'Escamoteur — Pontocho speakeasy, magician-bartender
OR Hello Dolly — check if Thu live jazz
Pontocho / Kiyamachi bar hop
Walk to hotel

title: Nara Day Trip / Hello Dolly Jazz

entries.text: Wake up
Iolite Coffee Roasters (hidden gem, Nakagyo)
Subway to Kyoto Station (4 min + walking)
Board Kintetsu Express to Nara (35 min, ¥760)
Arrive Kintetsu-Nara Station
Walk to Nara Park (5 min from Kintetsu station)
Buy deer crackers (200 yen/pack × 2), bow to deer, feed deer
Walk through park to Todai-ji Temple (20 min walk, flat ground)
Todai-ji — world's largest bronze Buddha (~600 yen)
Walk to Kasuga Taisha Shrine (15 min through deer park)
Kasuga Taisha — stone lanterns, forested path (~500 yen)
Walk to Naramachi historic district
⚠️ KICHI KICHI ATTEMPT #2 — both phones, 12:59, refresh at 1:00. Can do this from Nara!
Lunch — kakinoha-zushi (persimmon leaf sushi), Nara specialty (~1,000-1,500 yen)
Browse Naramachi
Walk to Kintetsu-Nara Station
Board Kintetsu Express back to Kyoto (35 min)
Arrive Kyoto Station
Subway/walk to Philosopher's Path area
Philosopher's Path — flat 2km canal walk, peaceful (fine for Brad's foot)
Nanzen-ji Temple — brick aqueduct photo op (free)
Walk/subway back to hotel area
Rest at hotel
Walk to Kyogoku Kaneyo
Dinner — kinshi-don (eel + egg omelet over rice, ~2,000-4,000 yen)
(B) Pontocho Fujita — kaiseki with river terrace
Walk to Hello Dolly on Pontocho
Hello Dolly — LIVE JAZZ FRIDAY! Sets at 8pm, 9:30pm ⚠️ TONIGHT IS THE NIGHT
Walk to Jazz Live Candy in Gion (10 min)
Jazz Live Candy — nightly live jazz
Walk to hotel (10 min)
SLOW MORNING — you're 9 days in
Wake up (or sleep in — no alarm)
Option A: Arashiyama
Hankyu from Kawaramachi to Arashiyama (25 min, ¥240)
Bamboo grove (quiet before 9am), Togetsukyo Bridge
Train back to hotel area
Option B: Slow morning
Coffee at % Arabica Higashiyama ⚠️ GO AT 8AM OPENING — GW lines 20-30 min by 10am
Walk along Kamo River, browse Teramachi arcades
Wander, rest, explore at your own pace
Option C: AMAM DACOTAN breakfast + Rakushaan pickup
Register for AMAM DACOTAN queue on Airwait (link on @amam_dacotan_kyoto IG) ⚠️ CHECK IG NIGHT BEFORE for closures
Called for entry (wander Karasuma Oike area while waiting, 10-15 min from hotel)
AMAM DACOTAN — artisan bakery in machiya. Must-grab: mentaiko baguette, rare doughnut. 2F cafe has % Arabica coffee. ~¥1-2k/pp. Cashless OK.
Pick up Rakushaan warabi mochi (Shichiku, 15 min from Kitaoji Stn) ⚠️ HAVE CONCIERGE CALL 075-494-2551 ON MAY 2 to reserve. Cash only, ¥1,500/16pc. Eat ASAP — texture degrades. Bring back to hotel.
Walk to Camellia Tea Ceremony location (Gion area)
⚠️ CAMELLIA TEA CEREMONY — BOOKED 12:00 PM — Matcha whisking, seasonal sweets, machiya setting (~45-60 min)
Walk to Izuju near Yasaka Shrine
Queue at Izuju (~15 min)
Eat saba-zushi — mackerel pressed sushi, Kyoto's signature ⚠️ BRAD'S MUST
Also try: Hiyoko Sushi nearby for incredible value omakase
⚠️ KICHI KICHI ATTEMPT — both phones, 12:59, refresh at 1:00. Day 3 of trying!
Walk through Gion daytime — different energy than dusk
Rest at hotel or Kodai-ji Temple (Zen gardens, ~600 yen)
Assemblages Kakimoto matcha parfait (if not done yesterday)
OPTION: Modern Haori Shopping Tour — all walkable in central Nakagyo-ku:
All within 10-15 min walk radius. Budget 2-3 hrs.
OR: Free time — pack for Osaka tomorrow, wander, rest
Walk toward Hikiniku to Come (near Kawaramachi)
Queue at Hikiniku to Come (~30-45 min) ⚠️ ARRIVE BY 5:45 before dinner rush
Eat charcoal-grilled hamburger steak — cult favorite
(B) Pontocho walk-in — find a kawayuka (river terrace) restaurant
Walk to P.M. Sounds (Kiyamachi, near hotel) — vinyl bar
OR L'Escamoteur (Pontocho) — magician-bartender speakeasy
Drinks + music
Hotel — pack for Osaka (luggage forwarded via takkyubin tomorrow AM)
Wake up
Weekenders Coffee Tominokoji — last Kyoto coffee
Return to hotel, finish packing, send luggage via takkyubin (front desk)
Check out Hotel Forza
Walk to Kawaramachi Station (2 min — right by hotel)
Board Hankyu Kyoto Line to Osaka-Umeda (43 min, ¥410)
Arrive Osaka-Umeda
Transfer to Midosuji Line, ride to Namba (10 min, ¥240)
Walk to Cross Hotel Osaka (4 min)
Check in, drop bags, freshen up
Walk to Mizuno okonomiyaki (Dotonbori, 5 min)
Queue at Mizuno (~30-45 min GW) ⚠️ ARRIVE BY 12:00-12:30
Eat okonomiyaki — mountain yam batter, incredibly fluffy
(B) Ajinoya — Bib Gourmand, less tourist-heavy
Walk back to hotel / freshen up
Lilo Coffee Roasters (walking distance, open til 11pm)
Subway Namba → Dobutsuen-mae (5 min) for Shinsekai
Explore Shinsekai — retro neon neighborhood
Yaekatsu kushikatsu (est. 1949) — don't double-dip! (~1,500-3,000 yen)
Tower Knives Osaka — knife gallery, free care lesson, name engraving
Subway back to Namba
Free time — rest, Dotonbori daytime stroll, browse shops
Subway to Kushikatsu Kei (~15 min)
Dinner — kushikatsu in renovated 80-year-old house, "phantom sauce"
(B) Yaekatsu again if you loved it and want more
Return to Namba area
Nakanoshima Festival — free! Handmade amusement park, food stalls
Walk Nakanoshima riverside (beautiful lit up at night)
Walk/subway back to hotel
Dotonbori night stroll — neon canal, Glico Running Man sign
Hotel
Wake up
Lilo Coffee (or hotel coffee for early caffeine)
Subway Namba → Tenjinbashisuji-Rokuchome (Midosuji + Tanimachi, 15 min)
Browse Tenjinbashi shopping arcade (Japan's longest)
Flower Mountain Tenjinbashi — Brad's shoe stop (5-8-27 Tenjinbashi)
Walk to Harukoma Sushi
Queue at Harukoma (~30-60 min GW) ⚠️ ARRIVE BY 10:30-11AM
Eat sushi — toro, uni, anago. Insane value (~1,300-2,000 yen)
Subway Tenjinbashi → Tanimachi-4-chome (10 min)
Osaka Castle — grounds, moat, castle tower (elevator, no stairs for Brad). ~600 yen
Subway back to Namba area
Choose your adventure:
A) Nakanoshima Festival Day 2 + riverside walk
B) Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade — 180 stores, tax-free
C) Momotaro Jeans Osaka (Horie neighborhood)
Takamura Wine & Coffee Roasters (opens 11am — afternoon visit)
Wrap up shopping/exploring
Subway Namba → Temma area (JR or metro, ~15 min)
Find Shoyu-Ramen Sodaisho (Temma, baseball-themed)
Eat chicken-clam shoyu ramen (~1,000 yen)
(B) Chukasoba Kazura (Awaza) — "mousse-like" broth
Walk/subway Temma → Nishi-Umeda (~10 min)
Arrive Billboard Live Osaka (Herbis Plaza ENT) — get seated, order drinks
🎵 CASIOPEA at Billboard Live Osaka ⚠️ TICKETS PURCHASED
Walk to Shinsaibashi or Nakanoshima night stroll (lit up riverside)
Bible Club Osaka — speakeasy nightcap (optional, walking distance from hotel)
Hotel
NO ALARM. IT'S YOUR BIRTHDAY.
Wake up naturally
Walk to Mel Coffee Roasters (opens 9am)
Birthday coffee — Melbourne-inspired, Probat roaster
Dotonbori daytime stroll — street food grazing, soak it in
Walk to Hozenji Yokocho — visit moss-covered Mizukake Fudo statue
Splash water for good luck on your birthday. Scout evening dinner route.
Walk/subway to Takotsubo takoyaki
5x Michelin-listed takoyaki — crispy exterior, molten center (~500-800 yen)
Walk to Dotonbori, grab any other street food that catches your eye
Return to hotel area
Birthday shopping:
Return to hotel, rest, get dressed for birthday dinner
Walk/taxi to Waldorf Astoria Osaka
Elevator to 28th floor
Canes & Tales — birthday cocktails, jazz, Osaka skyline sunset views
Walk/taxi to Hozenji Yokocho
Arrive at restaurant area — Hozenji Yokocho stroll
Matsuzaka Gyu Yakiniku M Hozenji-Hanare ⚠️ CONFIRMED 7:00 PM
BIRTHDAY DINNER — Matsuzaka wagyu, one of Japan's top 3 beef brands
Happy 40th, Brad. 🎂
Walk/subway to Kitashinchi (3 min from Kitashinchi Stn)

Ijou Maru Parfait — birthday dessert. Speakeasy art parfait bar (~¥4,500-5k each) ⚠️ FOLLOW @ijou_maru_parfait NOW for "membership." DM ~1 week ahead to reserve. Cashless only. Parfait revealed through glowing door. Perfect surreal birthday cap.
(B) If no Ijou Maru: Hozenji Yokocho stroll — find a tiny bar for a birthday nightcap
Option A: Bar Nayuta (Amerikamura, tiny door, no menu, til 3am)
Option B: Content birthday walk home along Dotonbori canal
Wake up
Lilo Coffee Roasters — one last Osaka coffee
Return to hotel, check out, grab bags (or already sent via takkyubin)
Walk to Namba Station (4 min)
Midosuji Line Namba → Shin-Osaka (15 min, ¥280)
Navigate to Shinkansen platform, buy snacks for the ride
Board Hikari 646 to Odawara ⚠️ BOOKED
Shinkansen to Odawara (2 hrs 20 min) — relax, read, nap
Arrive Odawara Station
Buy Hakone Free Pass at Odakyu counter (2-day, ¥6,000)
Optional: quick lunch at Odawara Station food court
Hakone Tozan Railway Odawara → Hakone-Yumoto (15 min)
Transfer at Hakone-Yumoto (wait for switchback train)
Hakone Tozan Railway Hakone-Yumoto → Gora (40 min, scenic switchbacks)
Walk to Hakone Gora Byakudan (or ryokan pickup from Gora Station)
Arrive ryokan
Check in (3pm official), get into yukata, explore ryokan
Private in-room onsen — YOU HAVE YOUR OWN. Soak. Decompress.
Rest, read, wander ryokan grounds
Get ready for dinner
Ryokan kaiseki dinner — multi-course, seasonal, ceremonial
Evening onsen soak — different light, different mood
Sleep. You've earned it.
Wake up
Traditional Japanese ryokan breakfast (included)
Morning onsen soak
Get dressed, prepare for day. Send luggage to Nippori via takkyubin (front desk).
Walk/bus from ryokan to Hakone Open-Air Museum (5 min, Free Pass)
Hakone Open-Air Museum — sculptures, Picasso, foot bath (2-2.5 hrs)
Walk/bus back to Gora Station
Cable car Gora → Sounzan (10 min, Free Pass)
Ropeway Sounzan → Owakudani (10 min, Free Pass)
Owakudani — volcanic valley, eat black eggs (¥500/5), Mt. Fuji views
Ropeway Owakudani → Togendai (15 min, Free Pass)
Walk to pirate ship pier at Togendai
Lake Ashi Pirate Ship — Togendai to Hakone-machi/Moto-Hakone (30 min, Free Pass)
Walk to Hakone Roasting Coffee (lakeside)
Coffee with panoramic lake views (3F Coffee & Vista)
Walk to bus stop
Bus back to Gora (30-40 min, Free Pass)
Walk to ryokan
Afternoon onsen + rest
Get ready for dinner
Ryokan kaiseki dinner — different menu from last night
Final onsen soak — savor it
Pack for tomorrow's departure to Tokyo
Sleep

title: Hakone → Tokyo / Pizza Bar Lunch + Sushi Yoshikawa Dinner

entries.text: Wake up
Quick morning onsen (last chance!)
Ryokan breakfast
Pack up, check out — luggage already sent via takkyubin
Hakone Tozan Railway Gora → Odawara (55 min, Free Pass)
Buy Shinkansen ticket at Odawara (or use SmartEX)
Shinkansen Odawara → Tokyo Station (35 min, ¥3,220)
Walk/taxi to Mandarin Oriental Tokyo (~10 min from Tokyo Station)
Buffer — freshen up, coffee in lobby
LUNCH — Pizza Bar on 38th ⚠️ 11:30 AM RESERVATION (¥15,000-20,000/pp)
Omakase pizza, #2 in the world, 8 seats, 38th floor Mandarin Oriental
Walk back to Tokyo Station
JR Yamanote Tokyo → Nippori (12 min, ¥170)
Walk to Hotel MyStays Nippori (3-5 min)
Check in, drop bags, freshen up
Yanaka Ginza — old-school shopping street, cat taiyaki, snacks
Missed spots flex time:
Travel back to Nippori area
Rest at hotel, get ready for last dinner
Travel to Sushi Yoshikawa (Ebisu)
Arrive early, settle in
DINNER — Sushi Yoshikawa omakase ⚠️ 8:30 PM RESERVATION (prepaid ¥11,000/pp)
Travel to evening bar spot
Last night drinks:
Head back to Nippori
Pack. Last night in Japan.
Wake up
Quick breakfast near hotel or hotel lobby
Walk to Nippori Station (5 min)
JR to Ueno (1 stop, 3 min)
Ameyoko Market — chaotic market under the tracks. Last-minute snacks + gifts.
Walk back through Yanaka Ginza — cat-tail donuts, taiyaki, nostalgia
Final stroll, last morning coffee
Return to hotel, collect luggage, check out
Walk to Nippori Station (5 min), find Keisei counter
Buy Skyliner ticket, grab any last konbini snacks
Board Keisei Skyliner ⚠️ 36 MIN DIRECT TO NARITA — all reserved
Arrive Narita Airport
Check in luggage (knives in CHECKED bags only)
Clear security + immigration
Duty-free shopping:
Kit-Kats, regional sweets, any final souvenirs
Gate area — relax, last Japanese konbini run, reflect on an incredible trip
Flight departs ✈️
